reasoning: The historic trend for this Liquidity and Credit Facilities series exhibits a substantial end-of-year seasonal spike due to predictable holiday and year-end funding needs. Last year, the level surged from around late November/early December into mid-late December (peaking rapidly over 20,000 around Dec 11) before gradually dissipating by mid-January and plunging back to a lower baseline by March. Since the forecast baseline date is Dec 7 (which reflects roughly the Dec 3 elevated value of 7,915), the mid-December resolution (Dec 14) has a high probability of capturing the peak of this seasonal surge, prompting a probability > 0.5. By early January (Jan 6), the values are typically winding down but may still hold residual elevation compared to early December, keeping this near a coin flip. By March (Mar 7), the seasonal needs will have entirely cleared, making a decrease relative to December more likely, though the long horizon invites uncertainty from potential new banking stresses or unexpected liquidity events, pulling the probability closer to 0.5 to reflect increased long-term horizon variance.
